arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ وَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلْ فَمَا بَلَّغْتَ رِسَالَتَهُۥ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْصِمُكَ مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ
translation: “O Messenger, convey what has been revealed to you from your Lord — and if you do not, you have not conveyed His message. And Allah will protect you from the people.”

shia.label: Shia emphasis
shia.body: Shia tafsir places it at Ghadīr Khumm: the message he must not withhold is ʿAlī’s wilāya, proclaimed as he raised ʿAlī’s hand before the pilgrims.

sunni.label: Sunni emphasis
sunni.body: Sunni tafsir reads it broadly: deliver the entire revelation, conceal nothing. After it came, the Prophet dismissed his guards — God now promised to protect him.

voiceover.hook: One verse tells the Prophet: deliver this one thing — or you've delivered nothing at all. What that 'one thing' is, two traditions read very differently.
voiceover.reference: Surah al-Maaidah, verse sixty-seven, revealed in Medina.
voiceover.payoff: Same words — for one, the whole of the revelation; for the other, the day of Ghadeer.
voiceover.question: Which of these two readings resonates with you

cta_text: Which of these two readings resonates with you?
caption_desc: If he held back one thing, the whole mission fails — but what was it? 🌙 #quran #tafsir #shia #sunni #ghadir
